natural_language: Here's the scene: a three-block tower already stands on the table. From bottom to top: A touches the table, B sits on A, and C caps the top. Only C is exposed and grabbable from this tower. Around it, four more blocks — D, E, F, and G — are each sitting alone on the table surface, nothing covering any of them. The gripper starts empty. Your job is extremely simple: just add G to the top of the existing tower. That's it. The final tower should read from bottom: A, B, C, and now G at the peak. The other three blocks (D, E, F) must stay exactly where they are, on the table, untouched. Do not move anything except G. I repeat: only G gets moved, everything else stays put.
